title: dentaria integrifolia var. californica
intro: Dentaria integrifolia var. californica, also known as California toothwort or California dentaria, is a perennial plant native to the western coast of North America. It is a member of the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and pain, as well as treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Dentaria integrifolia var. californica include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water, as well as tinctures and salves.
